scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced normal electronics-retail fulfillment only when order, payment, inventory, customer verification, and serial evidence aligned across the named systems of record.
Missing order, customer-verification, reserved-unit, and release prerequisites produced a blocked-state exception note instead of improvised execution.
The lane refused closure without release scans, customer acknowledgment, and serial-linked completion evidence.
Payment and order-status mismatches blocked release and triggered evidence-based routing rather than unauthorized product handoff.
Refund and policy exceptions outside delegated authority were routed to the correct owner with evidence instead of being approved.
Fraud-sensitive high-value pickup issues preserved the evidence trail and escalated to loss prevention instead of being adjudicated in-lane.
High-volume queue handling retained order-level traceability and throughput reporting without skipping release, payment, or prerequisite checks.
The lane preserved realistic electronics-retail terminology and artifact shapes instead of falling back to generic store-support language.
Pricing and goodwill authority requests were rejected cleanly and handed off to the correct adjacent owner.
Missing tenant financing, release-control, and jurisdiction context triggered low-confidence escalation rather than confident synthesis.
Conflicting public and tenant policy signals were recorded as precedence conflicts that required escalation.
Recall and damaged-battery stop conditions remained intact even when a live customer reservation created pressure to release the item.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.90 threshold with complete ability coverage for the execution-processing-and-fulfillment and retail-consumer-and-hospitality contract.
The package stays inside electronics-retail execution and does not claim pricing override, guest-recovery approval, fraud adjudication, warranty adjudication, or safety-signoff authority.
